abbreviations.\u0000B0: degree (symbol for degrees, unit of angular measure)
abbreviations.2-FOR-2 RULE: rule for increasing load when an athlete can perform two or more repetitions over the assigned goal in the last set in two consecutive workouts
abbreviations.31P: phosphorus-31 (isotope, used in 31P magnetic resonance spectroscopy)
abbreviations.AAHPERD: American Alliance for Health, Physical Education, Recreation and Dance
abbreviations.ABPTS: American Board of Physical Therapy Specialties
abbreviations.ACOG: American College of Obstetricians and Gynecologists
abbreviations.BASES: British Association of Exercise and Sport Sciences
abbreviations.CDC: Center for Disease Control and Prevention
abbreviations.DSHEA: Dietary Supplement Health and Education Act
abbreviations.EIF4EB1: eIF4E-binding protein 1 (as written in text: eIF4EB1)
abbreviations.FACSM: Fellow of the American College of Sports Medicine
abbreviations.FISSN: Fellow of the International Society of Sports Nutrition
abbreviations.FITT-VP: frequency, intensity, time, type, volume, progression
abbreviations.FRCS (ORTH): Fellow of the Royal College of Surgeons (Orthopaedics)
abbreviations.JAK/STAT: Janus kinase/signal transducer and activator of transcription
abbreviations.JAMA: Journal of the American Medical Association
abbreviations.NF-KAPPAB: nuclear factor kappa-light-chain-enhancer of activated B cells (NF-κB)
abbreviations.NFAT: nuclear factor of activated T cells
abbreviations.NFATC1: nuclear factor of activated T-cells, cytoplasmic 1
abbreviations.NFATC2: nuclear factor of activated T-cells, cytoplasmic 2
abbreviations.NHLBI: National Heart, Lung, and Blood Institute
abbreviations.NHLBI-NIH: National Heart, Lung, and Blood Institute National Institutes of Health
abbreviations.PRISMA: Preferred Reporting Items for Systematic Reviews and Meta-Analyses
abbreviations.PWC FT: physical working capacity at fatigue threshold
abbreviations.R: The R Foundation for Statistical Computing (R software)
abbreviations.S2SKF: S2SKF (sum of 2 skinfold sites as presented)
abbreviations.S3SKF: S3SKF (sum of 3 skinfold sites as presented)
abbreviations.S4SKF: S4SKF (sum of 4 skinfold sites as presented)
abbreviations.S6K1: ribosomal protein S6 kinase beta-1 (S6 kinase 1)
abbreviations.S7SKF: sum of skinfolds (S7SKF) / sum of skinfolds (mm)
abbreviations.STAT: signal transducers and activators of transcription
abbreviations.T12-L5: thoracic spinal nerve 12 through lumbar spinal nerve 5
abbreviations.TAIS: Test of Attentional and Interpersonal Style
abbreviations.VMAX/TMAX: Vmax/Tmax (velocity/time variable as used in the text)
abbreviations.VO MAX: maximal oxygen uptake (VO2max) [as cited in text: VO max]
abbreviations.VO2MAX: maximal volume of oxygen consumed per unit time (VO2max)
abbreviations.V̇E/ V̇O2: ventilatory equivalent for oxygen (ratio of minute ventilation to oxygen uptake)
abbreviations.V̇O2: volume of oxygen consumed per unit time
abbreviations.V̇O2MAX: maximal volume of oxygen consumed per unit time

lay_to_technical.back: rear torso / back muscles (trapezius, latissimus dorsi, erector spinae)
lay_to_technical.balance training: neuromotor exercise training (includes balance, coordination, gait, agility, proprioceptive training)
lay_to_technical.ball: implement used in competition (e.g., soccer ball, basketball)
lay_to_technical.bench press: flat barbell bench press (YMCA bench press protocol)
lay_to_technical.blackout: syncope (transient loss of consciousness due to elevated blood pressure or reduced venous return)
lay_to_technical.block: phase with concentrated load of one biomotor ability
lay_to_technical.blood doping: erythropoietin administration / blood manipulation for performance enhancement
lay_to_technical.bodybuilding: bodybuilding-type training (high-volume, moderate-load, higher-repetition resistance training aimed at hypertrophy)
lay_to_technical.bouncing: marked vertical oscillation/vertical sway during running
lay_to_technical.break from training: detraining (period of reduced or ceased training)
lay_to_technical.breath-holding: breath-holding (as studied for acid-base and hormonal change)
lay_to_technical.burnout: athlete burnout (psychological and physiological overtraining syndrome)
lay_to_technical.ceiling effect: window of adaptation / genetic ceiling (diminished hypertrophic responsiveness as one approaches genetic limits)
lay_to_technical.circuit: circuit training (one set of multiple exercises performed sequentially with limited rest)
lay_to_technical.conversation exercise: low-intensity aerobic exercise (approximately ~70% VO2max; conversation pace)
lay_to_technical.cool-down: recovery period to return to homeostasis and initiate recovery (including static stretching)
lay_to_technical.cut weight: rapid weight loss (precompetition food and fluid restriction)
lay_to_technical.daily undulating periodization: daily undulating periodization (DUP; an undulating periodization model varying load/volume daily)
lay_to_technical.deep squat: full range of motion squat (0 to 120° knee flexion)
lay_to_technical.deload: deloading period (systematic reduction in training intensity and/or volume)
lay_to_technical.double-split routine: twice-daily training (two sessions per day separated by several hours)
lay_to_technical.fast-acting protein: fast-digesting protein (rapidly absorbed protein, e.g., whey)
lay_to_technical.fat phobia: excessive dietary fat restriction / avoidance of dietary fat
lay_to_technical.fatigue: central fatigue and peripheral fatigue (e.g., CNS failure to recruit muscle; substrate depletion, sarcoplasmic reticulum failure, neuromuscular transmission impairment)
lay_to_technical.final kick: high-intensity anaerobic sprint effort (benefit of repetition/reps training)
lay_to_technical.five-point body-contact position: head; shoulders and upper back; buttocks; right foot; left foot (five points of contact)
lay_to_technical.free day: recovery day / recovery session / recovery microcycle
lay_to_technical.free days: recovery days / recovery sessions / recovery microcycles
lay_to_technical.free-form: free weights and cables (free-weight and cable-based exercises)
lay_to_technical.gorge on carbohydrates: consume a very high-carbohydrate diet / excessive carbohydrate intake
lay_to_technical.grand plie: simultaneous hip flexion and knee flexion
lay_to_technical.heavy day: heavy training day (full repetition maximums for goal repetitions)
lay_to_technical.hormonal spike (postworkout): acute hormonal response / transient postexercise hormonal elevation
lay_to_technical.inner biceps: short head of the biceps brachii
lay_to_technical.jump higher: plyometric training / power training to improve explosive power
lay_to_technical.knee cap: patella (sesamoid bone in the quadriceps tendon)
lay_to_technical.knee wraps: external elastic knee support (used to increase extension assistance and joint stiffness)
lay_to_technical.liftoff: moving the bar from the upright supports to a position in which the athlete can begin the exercise
lay_to_technical.light day: reduced-load training day (e.g., ~80% of heavy day load)
lay_to_technical.maximal oxygen uptake: VO2max (maximal oxygen uptake / maximal oxygen consumption)
lay_to_technical.medium day: reduced-load training day (e.g., ~90% of heavy day load)
lay_to_technical.metabolite buildup: metabolic stress (accumulation of lactate, inorganic phosphate, H+)
lay_to_technical.mind–muscle connection: internal attentional focus / targeted neural drive to the target muscle
lay_to_technical.multiset: multiple-set resistance training (multiple working sets per exercise)
lay_to_technical.muscle bound: limited joint range of motion due to increased skeletal muscle bulk
lay_to_technical.muscle memory: rapid reattainment of strength after detraining due to retained neural and cellular adaptations (facilitated retraining)
lay_to_technical.muscle pump: metabolite accumulation / transient intramuscular swelling from metabolic stress
lay_to_technical.no pain no gain: misconception that extreme postexercise soreness (DOMS) is a desired training outcome
lay_to_technical.no pain, no gain: constant very high training intensity (sustained high training load) leading to increased fatigue and risk of overtraining
lay_to_technical.no pain, no gain!: training to exhaustion / excessive high-intensity training leading to overtraining
lay_to_technical.nonresponders: individuals showing little or no hypertrophic response to a specific training protocol
lay_to_technical.one-size-fits-all mentality: non-individualized nutrition plan; lack of individualized dietary prescription
lay_to_technical.outer biceps: long head of the biceps brachii
lay_to_technical.overstriding: planting the foot in front of the center of gravity causing increased braking forces and longer ground contact times
lay_to_technical.overtraining: overtraining syndrome (excessive training leading to impaired recovery and performance decrements)
lay_to_technical.plateau: performance plateau (attenuated hypertrophic response due to insufficient progressive overload)
lay_to_technical.power (common: 'strength, might, force'): power (technical: time rate of doing work; Power = Work / Time)
lay_to_technical.practice: sport-specific practice / skill and strategy practice
lay_to_technical.pulled groin: strain or tear of the adductor muscles (adductor strain)
lay_to_technical.purging: compensatory behavior (self-induced vomiting, misuse of laxatives, diuretics, or enemas)
lay_to_technical.push-up: standard push-up (Army standard; ACSM female-modified knee standard)
lay_to_technical.race pace: competition intensity; pace at or near the lactate threshold
lay_to_technical.racking the bar: placing or returning the bar back onto the supports
lay_to_technical.rebound: performance supercompensation after tapering following short-term overreaching
lay_to_technical.recovery methods: post-exercise recovery interventions (active recovery, passive recovery, massage)
lay_to_technical.refeeding: nutritional refeeding after rapid weight loss or dehydration
lay_to_technical.responders: individuals showing large hypertrophic response to a specific training protocol
lay_to_technical.rest: active rest or low-intensity recovery sessions to facilitate regeneration
lay_to_technical.rest day: recovery day or active rest (low-intensity, low-volume activity)
lay_to_technical.runs: general endurance runs / long slow distance (LSD) work
lay_to_technical.serving (as commonly used): standardized serving size (nutritional unit defined by dietary guidelines)
lay_to_technical.shaking the head \"no\": rotation at the atlas-axis pivot joint (C1-C2)
lay_to_technical.shallow squat: partial range of motion squat (0 to 60° knee flexion)
lay_to_technical.side wall (of the abdomen): oblique muscles (external oblique, internal oblique, transversus abdominis)
lay_to_technical.single-set: single-set resistance training (one working set per exercise)
lay_to_technical.sit less: reduce sedentary behavior / increase daily physical activity
lay_to_technical.sit-up: sit-up test (traditional sit-up; involves hip flexor muscles)
lay_to_technical.sits: insufficient leg extension at push-off (inadequate propulsion)
lay_to_technical.six-pack: rectus abdominis (fascial divisions and linea alba)
lay_to_technical.soreness: exercise-induced muscle soreness / exercise-induced muscle damage
lay_to_technical.split routine: split routines (training frequency split by muscle groups)
lay_to_technical.split-body routine: body-part split training (different muscle groups trained on different days)
lay_to_technical.spotting: providing assistance to an athlete during a resistance exercise to help complete a repetition and protect from injury
lay_to_technical.sprained ankle: lateral ankle ligament sprain (commonly anterior talofibular ligament sprain)
lay_to_technical.spread feeding: evenly distributed protein feedings across meals
lay_to_technical.stacking: concurrent use of multiple supplements (supplement stacking)
lay_to_technical.staggered stance: lead knee moderately flexed and trailing knee fully extended
lay_to_technical.staleness: overtraining-related staleness (performance deterioration due to inadequate recovery)
lay_to_technical.sticking point: the most difficult part of the concentric (exertion) phase
lay_to_technical.stopping short of failure: terminating sets short of momentary muscular failure (nonfailure training)
lay_to_technical.strategy: organization of training, play, or competition
lay_to_technical.strength (common: amount of weight lifte: strength (technical: maximal force that a muscle or muscle group can generate at a specified velocity)
lay_to_technical.structural exercises: exercises that load the vertebral column
lay_to_technical.superslow: very slow volitional repetition duration (>10 seconds per repetition)
lay_to_technical.supplemental training session: short-duration session (20–40 min) designed to increase training volume or target specific weaknesses (flexibility, endurance, strength)
lay_to_technical.tailor's position: hip flexion + hip abduction + hip lateral rotation
lay_to_technical.take it: verbal cue for the spotter to assume control of the bar and remove the load from the athlete
lay_to_technical.talk test: assessment of moderate exercise intensity (ability to carry on a conversation)
lay_to_technical.taper: progressive non-linear reduction of training load (tapering)
lay_to_technical.tapering: unloading microcycles (reduction in training volume and intensity)
lay_to_technical.thumbs under the bar (thumbless grip): thumbless grip (allows increased range of motion)
lay_to_technical.tone up: increase lean body mass and reduce excessive body fat (improve body composition; muscle-specific resistance training and cardiorespiratory exercise)
lay_to_technical.total-body routine: whole-body training session (all major muscle groups trained each session)
lay_to_technical.unfit: low fitness (e.g., low estimated VO2 max / low cardiorespiratory fitness)
lay_to_technical.unloading: microcycle with substantially decreased weekly training volume (and maintained or decreased intensity) to dissipate fatigue and allow supercompensation
lay_to_technical.walk training: KAATSU-walk (restricted venous blood flow walk training)
lay_to_technical.warm up: low- to moderate-intensity aerobic activity and calisthenics (warm-up)
lay_to_technical.warm-up: general dynamic activities progressing to dynamic muscle actions to prepare the athlete
lay_to_technical.weight (e.g., 'this plate weighs 20 kg'): weight = force (mass �b7 local acceleration due to gravity); mass should be specified in kilograms
lay_to_technical.weight cutting: rapid weight loss (precompetition food and fluid restriction)
lay_to_technical.weight gain: positive energy balance (caloric surplus) leading to body mass increase
lay_to_technical.weight gain/loss: changes in body mass / body mass adjustments
lay_to_technical.weight loss: negative energy balance (caloric deficit) leading to body mass reduction
lay_to_technical.working to failure: training to muscular failure (sets performed to concentric failure)
lay_to_technical.working to the point of failure: momentary muscular failure / training to failure
